prompt: Please read the README.md file in the current directory and tell me what it contains.

availableCommands.name: memory refresh
availableCommands.description: Refreshes the memory from the source.

availableCommands.name: memory list
availableCommands.description: Lists the paths of the GEMINI.md files in use.

availableCommands.name: memory inbox
availableCommands.description: Lists skills extracted from past sessions that are pending review.

availableCommands.name: extensions install
availableCommands.description: Install an extension from a git repo or local path.

availableCommands.name: extensions link
availableCommands.description: Link an extension from a local path.

availableCommands.name: init
availableCommands.description: Analyzes the project and creates a tailored GEMINI.md file

availableCommands.name: restore
availableCommands.description: Restore to a previous checkpoint, or list available checkpoints to restore. This will reset the conversation and file history to the state it was in when the checkpoint was created

content.text:
**Examining File Content**

I'm now zeroing in on the task at hand. The primary goal is clear: to get the contents of the `README.md` file. My current strategy is to utilize the `read_file` tool to achieve this efficiently.
`README.md` file describes HAPI, a tool that allows users to run official
AI agent sessions (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode) locally and control them

remotely via a Web, PWA, or Telegram Mini App.

Key features include:
*   **Seamless Handoff:** Work locally and switch to remote

control without losing context.
*   **Native First:** Wraps existing AI agents, maintaining the same terminal experience.
*   **AFK Without Stopping:** Approve AI requests from

a phone.
*   **Your AI, Your Choice:** Supports various AI models with a unified workflow.
*   **Terminal Anywhere:** Run commands from a phone or browser connected to the working machine.
*   **Voice Control:** Built

-in voice assistant for hands-free operation.
*   **Workspace Browser:** Opt-in feature to browse a scoped file tree and start sessions in subdirectories.

The `README.md` also provides a demo link, getting

started instructions using `npx @twsxtd/hapi hub --relay` or `npx @twsxtd/hapi`, and notes on E2E encryption via WireGuard + TLS. It also points to documentation for installation, app details, how it works, Cursor Agent, Voice Assistant, Why
HAPI, and FAQ. Finally, it includes instructions for building from source (`bun install`, `bun run build:single-exe`) and credits the original "Happy" project.
